zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Massey於1612年12月出生在英國爾伯頓時，他的父親Sir William Massey of Puddington32歲，母親Catherine Herbert32歲。他於大約1650年與Alice Braithwait在英國爾伯頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1674年1月11日在英國爾伯頓享年61歲，埋葬在英國爾伯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Massey于1612年12月出生在英国爾伯頓时，他的父亲Sir William Massey of Puddington32岁，母亲Catherine Herbert32岁。他于大约1650年与Alice Braithwait在英国爾伯頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1674年1月11日在英国爾伯頓享年61岁，埋葬在英国爾伯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Massey was born in December 1612, in Burton, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir William Massey of Puddington, was 32 and his mother, Catherine Herbert, was 32. He married Alice Braithwait about 1650, in Burton, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 11 January 1674, in Burton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Burton, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Massey est né en décembre 1612, à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Massey of Puddington, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Catherine Herbert, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Alice Braithwait environ 1650, à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 11 janvier 1674, à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Massey im Dezember 1612 in Burton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Massey of Puddington 32 und seine Mutter Catherine Herbert 32. Er hat um 1650 in Burton, Cheshire, England Alice Braithwait geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 11. Januar 1674 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Burton, Cheshire, England und wurde in Burton, Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Massey è nato il nel dicembre 1612 nel Burton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir William Massey of Puddington, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Herbert, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Alice Braithwait circa 1650, nel Burton, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 11 gennaio 1674, nel Burton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Burton, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Masseyが1612年12月にイギリスチェシュアイアブルトンで生まれた時，父Sir William Massey of Puddingtonは32歳，母Catherine Herbertは32歳であった。約1650年にイギリスチェシュアイアブルトンでAlice Braithwaitと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1674年1月11日にイギリスチェシュアイアブルトンで61歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアブルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Massey(이)가 1612년 12월 영국 체실르 버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Massey of Puddington(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Herbert(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1650년 영국 체실르 버턴에서 Alice Braithwait(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1674년 1월 11일 영국 체실르 버턴에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 버턴에 묻혔다.

mn.December 1612–11 January 1674: 1612 оны Арванхоёрдугаар сар–1674 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 11

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Massey nasceu em dezembro de 1612, em Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Massey of Puddington, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Herbert, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Braithwait aproximadamente 1650, em Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de janeiro de 1674, em Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Massey родился декабрь 1612 г. в местности буртон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Massey of Puddington было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Catherine Herbert, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Braithwait около 1650 г. в местности буртон, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 11 января 1674 г. в местности буртон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности буртон, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Massey nació en diciembre de 1612, en Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Massey of Puddington, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Catherine Herbert, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Alice Braithwait aproximadamente 1650, en Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 11 de enero de 1674, en Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
